name: kanban
description:
Organize your task board in Markdown, right next to your code. Claude proposes the next work, writes the cards, and archives what's done — you steer the backlog in plain language from the terminal. The board lives in docs/kanban/: in git, diffable, readable by both you and the agent. No database, no web app, no MCP.

Example 1: say "what's next?" — Claude reads the board and your sources and proposes 3 new tasks.
Example 2: say "add a task: …" — Claude reviews the idea, writes a clear card, and adds it to the index.
Example 3: say "dive deeper on #4" — Claude pushes card #4 one stage toward a concrete plan.
Example 4: say "#4 is done" — Claude compresses it into the archive and removes the card.
